title: Cynanchum maritimum
intro: Cynanchum maritimum, also known as maritime Cynanchum, is a perennial plant species in the Apocynaceae family, commonly found in coastal areas. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, as well as its ability to protect against cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum maritimum include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, such as maritidine, cynanchine, and quercetin. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, including extracts, teas, and capsules, which are used to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Cynanchum maritimum is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Cynanchum, which includes various species of milkweeds and related plants. The plant's classification is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Gentianales, family - Apocynaceae, genus - Cynanchum, species - C. maritimum. The classification of C. maritimum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family. Understanding the plant's classification is essential for further study and identification.
